delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
In this course, students will work in groups explaining/presenting scientific ideas/topics related to the environment/disasters weekly in English.
Through using English group communication/presentation skills, students will develop their ideas into casual and formal class presentations.
Students will develop practical communicative presentation competence & learn to express more scientific ideas in English.
Students are expected to explain/communicate & give SHORT PRESENTATIONS expressing themselves using technical/environmental/disaster-related ENGLISH.
Students are expected to learn to THINK & GIVE simple scientific answers using ENGLISH.
Lesson content used in class will also be posted on moodle.
Classes are basically delivered live in class, but sometimes may be on-demand.
aims: Have fun improving your English communication/presentation skills by organizing and expressing complex scientific and technical information in clear and simple English.
goals:
Students will be able to convey complex technical data in English using basic information structures.
Students will develop practical communicative presentation competence & learn to express more scientific ideas in English.

Students will be able to create English language presentations.
Students will be able to explain/communicate & give SHORT PRESENTATIONS expressing themselves using technical/environmental/disaster-related ENGLISH.

Students will be able to have simple, informal conversations which are important to building international relationships.
Students will understand and be able to communicate about emergencies, disasters and the environment in English.

plan.text:
Introduction/ Class Orientation
Description & Information Mapping
Written/Spoken Self-introduction about your interest in the environment/disasters/disaster prevention

Environmentalists/Environment/Disaster History PRESENTATION [5%]
Search the Internet for data on history of disasters/environmentalists
Preparation/Research: choose one thing of interest for presentation
Oral & Written Presentation [5%] Using Description & Information Mapping
Look at audience
Point at info-map
Do NOT read!

Sequence
In groups (individually) make an info-map & sentences on environmental/disaster sequence of events

Environmental/Disaster Sequence Presentation [10%]
Good/correct pronunciation
Look at audience
Point at info-map
Do NOT read

Cause & Effect
In groups (individually) make an info-map & sentences on environmental/disaster cause & effect systems/events
SDGs life below water/life on land

Cause & Effect Presentation [10%]
Loud clear voice
Look at audience
Point at info-map
Do NOT read

Inference
In groups (individually) make drawing (an info-map) & sentences on environmental/disaster inference of events/mystery
SDGs reduce inequality

Inference MIDTERM EXAM Presentation [20%]
Loud clear voice/good pronunciation
Look at audience/Do NOT read
Point at info-map/drawing
Relax

Comparison
Do group (individual) comparison research on Comparison
Do group (individual) comparison research on environmental/disaster systems/events

Comparison Presentation [10%]
Loud clear voice/good pronunciation
Look at audience/Do NOT read
Point at info-map/drawing
Relax

Graphs & Data Presentation [10%]
Loud clear voice/good pronunciation
Look at audience/Do NOT read
Point at graph
Relax

Pros & Cons [Final Group (Individual) EXAM Presentation]
Topic: PROS & CONS of environmental/disaster/scientific related thing/event/question
SDGs clean water & sanitation/climate action/sustainable cities & communities/affordable clean energy

Review/Prepare FINAL EXAM PRESENTATION: Pros & Cons [Human vs. AI]
Topic: PROS & CONS of environmental/disaster/scientific related thing/event/question

Review/Prepare/Do FINAL Group (individual) EXAM PRESENTATION [25%]
Topic: PROS & CONS of environmental/disaster/scientific related thing/event/question
(SDGs clean water & sanitation/climate action/sustainable cities & communities etc.)
Oral PRESENTATION & Written Report [25%]
Loud clear voice/good pronunciation
Look at audience/Do NOT read
Point at info-map/drawing/chart
Relax

Do FINAL Group (individual) EXAM PRESENTATION [25%]
Topic: PROS & CONS of environmental/disaster/scientific related thing/event/question
(SDGs clean water & sanitation/climate action/sustainable cities & communities etc.)
Oral PRESENTATION & Written Report [25%]
Loud clear voice/good pronunciation
Look at audience/Do NOT read
Point at info-map/drawing/chart
Relax

textbooks: Original class materials will be posted on the KU moodle site [https://moodle.kochi-u.ac.jp/2026/] or handed out in class.
prereq:
Successful completion of basic general education Eikaiwa I & II (speaking/conversation) as a 1st-year student & a Science major.
The course is taught in English at a Post Elementary level. It is assumed that students have some basic English communication skills, so students must be ready to try to function in English and improve on their basic communication/presentation skills.
prep:
Students are expected to practice English skills and prepare for all classes.
Practice outside of the classroom is essential.
teachers: ◎ フェビアン ポーラ ダイアン

50%: 20% In-class Work/Participation/Effort/Attitude [Maximum of 3 absences]
30% Homework/preparation [moodle]
50%: 30% Presentations
20% EXAM PRESENTATION

extra.label: 教員の実務経験の有無
extra.text:
有
教員の実務経験との関連
Have worked as a teacher; teaching in kindergarten, elementary school NPO summer programs, junior & senior high school, vocational schools, cram schools, language schools & university. Before teaching, I worked for a small company in public relations/marketing & advertising. Over the years have volunteered or worked in/with NPO groups.

その他
Some classes will be online or on-demand moodle
